Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.

Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 38 of about 43 target actions so far.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 37 actions returned the board to a state already seen 37 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 37 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game over, level not completed. Let me check current state and try to understand what happened.
# The game is over but MOUSE is still valid. Let me try clicking to see if game restarts.

# First, let me check the current grid state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find bottom-right grid cells (red and blue)
br_cells = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b') and 35 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 60:
        # Get center of cell
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        center_r = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_c = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        br_cells.append((n['color'], center_r, center_c, n['pixels']))

# Sort by position
br_cells.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] // 8 * 8, x[2] // 8 * 8))
print("Bottom-right grid cells:", br_cells)

# Check orange bar
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"Orange bar: {n['pixels']} pixels, boundary={n['boundary'][:2]}")

# Try clicking to see if game restarts
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 39}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"MOUSE_on_brg_cell": "Clicking toggles cell color in bottom-right grid (blue↔red)", "MOUSE_on_bracket": "No effect", "MOUSE_on_other_grid": "No effect", "MOUSE_on_center": "No effect", "MOUSE_on_frame": "No effect"}, "object_taxonomy": {"grid_cell": "6x6 colored square with 2-char gaps", "center_marker": "gray-bordered white with red 2x2 (position varies)", "charcoal_frame": "Border around bottom-right grid", "gray_bracket": "Gray L-shaped corner brackets", "orange_bar": "Bar at row 63, grows with actions"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Orange bar at row 63 - grows with action count"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {"level_1": "FAILED - game_over after 38 actions, tried many patterns"}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Game over after 38 actions. Level 1 not completed. Bottom-right grid is now all blue.", "goal_model": "Unknown - need to restart or find new approach", "recent_findings": ["Game over triggered after many failed pattern attempts"], "open_questions": ["Can game be restarted?", "What is the correct pattern?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking to restart, then try position-3 pattern", "strategy_log": ["Tried all 3 source patterns", "Tried XOR, AND, majority vote, even-count, all-red, NOR", "All failed, game over"], "failed_probes": ["All source patterns", "XOR, AND, majority vote, even-count, all-red, NOR patterns", "Center click, bracket click, other grid click, frame click"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game over, level not completed. Let me check current state and try to understand what happened.
# The game is over but MOUSE is still valid. Let me try clicking to see if game restarts.

# First, let me check the current grid state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find bottom-right grid cells (red and blue)
br_cells = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b') and 35 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 60:
        # Get center of cell
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        center_r = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_c = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        br_cells.append((n['color'], center_r, center_c, n['pixels']))

# Sort by position
br_cells.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] // 8 * 8, x[2] // 8 * 8))
print("Bottom-right grid cells:", br_cells)

# Check orange bar
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"Orange bar: {n['pixels']} pixels, boundary={n['boundary'][:2]}")

# Try clicking to see if game restarts
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 39}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Level 1 not completed. Bottom-right grid is now all blue.", "goal_model": "Unknown - need to restart or find new approach", "recent_findings": ["Game over triggered after many failed pattern attempts"], "open_questions": ["Can game be restarted?", "What is the correct pattern?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking to restart, then try position-3 pattern", "strategy_log": ["Tried all 3 source patterns", "Tried XOR, AND, majority vote, even-count, all-red, NOR", "All failed, game over"], "failed_probes": ["All source patterns", "XOR, AND, majority vote, even-count, all-red, NOR patterns", "Center click, bracket click, other grid click, frame click"]}}
